scheme_name: Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for assistance to MSMEs: Assistance for Patent Registration
page_title: Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for assistance to MSMEs: Assistance for Patent Registration

details: The "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for MSMEs" is an umbrella scheme launched by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat. It aims to empower the state's dynamic MSME sector, which significantly contributes to employment, GDP, and exports. Effective from October 5, 2022, to October 4, 2027, this initiative supports growth, investments, and the vision of "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat" and "Aatmanirbhar Bharat."

The component "Assistance for Patent Registration", provides financial support for individuals and legal entities to obtain patent registration for their developed products or processes. It covers a portion of the costs incurred for patent application, attorney fees, and associated expenses during the scheme's operative period.
benefits: 75% of the cost incurred for patent applications.
Fees for patent attorney, patent service center, and patent registration.
Expenses related to patent equipment development (excluding travel and hotel costs).
Domestic patent attorney fees are capped at ₹50,000/-.
International patent attorney fees are capped at ₹2,00,000/- per country.
50% of assistance disbursed after the publication/notification of the patent.
The remaining 50% will be disbursed after the issuance of the patent certificate.
eligibility: Micro/Small/Medium enterprises or Individuals are eligible for assistance under the scheme.
The enterprise must have obtained acknowledgment or registration from the Ministry of MSME or the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), as applicable.
The applicant shall have to submit application within one year from the date of publication/notification of the patent.

online: portal. Click on "New Investor Registration" to access the registration form.
Fill in the Registration Form. Click on "Submit".
Verify the E-mail ID using the link sent on your registered E-mail ID.
After verification, you will be registered successfully.
Enter the email address and password you used during registration.
Click the "Login" button to access your account.
Now fill in the required details and mandatory documents about your business to complete the application process.

documents_required: Particulars or constitution of the applicant/ patent holder.
Copy of PAN Card of Enterprise.
Copy of PAN Card of Applicant/Promoters/Directors/partners and authorised signatory.
Copy of application submitted to patent office along with Form 2.
Acknowledgement receipt for filing application for patent registration.
Payment receipts of fees paid to patent office.
Invoices and Payment receipts of fees paid to patent attorney.
List of equipment purchased for developing patent with justification and bills of the same.

CA Certified Detailed statement of expenditure for obtaining patent registration, as per the format specified in Annexure (For National patent, International patent, and country-wise patent send separate C.A. certified Statement).
Highlighted Bank account statement of expenditure done for patent.
Copy of CA Certified Balance Sheet of the last financial year./Annual Audit report. (if applicable).
Copy of Board Resolution/Consent Letter/Power of Attorney. (if applicable).

faqs.question: What is the "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for Assistance to MSMEs"?
faqs.answer: The “Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for Assistance to MSMEs” is a state initiative designed to support and strengthen the MSME sector in Gujarat. It aims to enhance growth, encourage investments, and contribute to the vision of self-reliant Gujarat and India.

faqs.question: When is the scheme operative?
faqs.answer: The scheme is operative from October 5, 2022, to October 4, 2027, covering a period of five years.

faqs.question: What are the objectives of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to boost MSME growth, enhance the industrial ecosystem, encourage investments, and contribute to Gujarat’s and India’s self-reliance vision.

faqs.question: What are some initiatives included in the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme includes the establishment of new industrial estates, sector-specific parks, and infrastructure projects, along with amendments to the Gujarat Industrial Policy 2020 to further support MSME growth.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to benefit from the scheme?
faqs.answer: MSMEs established and operating within Gujarat are eligible to benefit from the scheme, as per the criteria defined in the policy framework.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the "Assistance for Patent Registration" component?
faqs.answer: Individuals or any legal entity can apply for assistance under the scheme.

faqs.question: What is the maximum amount of assistance available under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The maximum assistance is 75% of the total cost, up to ₹25,00,000/- per applicant or enterprise.

faqs.question: What costs are covered under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers costs related to patent attorney fees, patent service centers, patent registration, and equipment purchased to develop the patent (excluding travel and hotel charges).

faqs.question: What is the cap on attorney fees?
faqs.answer: Attorney fees for national patents are capped at ₹50,000/-, and for international patents, the fee is capped at ₹2,00,000/- per country.

faqs.question: When will the assistance be disbursed?
faqs.answer: 50% of the assistance will be disbursed after the publication or notification of the patent, and the remaining 50% will be given after the patent certificate is issued.

faqs.question: How long does an applicant have to submit the application for assistance?
faqs.answer: The application must be submitted within one year from the date of publication or notification of the patent.

faqs.question: Are there any expenditures that are not eligible for assistance?
faqs.answer: Travel, hotel charges, and other unrelated expenses are not eligible for assistance.

faqs.question: Can enterprises that applied for the scheme under previous guidelines receive assistance?
faqs.answer: Yes, eligible enterprises that opted for this scheme under previous guidelines can also claim assistance for expenditures incurred during that period.

faqs.question: Can an enterprise receive assistance for more than one patent?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme provides assistance for any number of patent applications, subject to the ₹25,00,000/- ceiling per applicant/enterprise.

faqs.question: Can expenses for developing patent equipment be reimbursed?
faqs.answer: Yes, expenses related to patent equipment development are eligible for reimbursement, except for travel and hotel charges.
